description: Counterpart of the exam-style case agents-exam/adv-says-wf-is-agent.json (false friend: says 'workflow', behavior is chat-based Q&A). Gold mirrors the exam case (agent-anchored, either embeds value). NOTE: a chat-trigger workflow with an AI Agent node is n8n's long-established pattern for this request — if real builds consistently go that way while the exam grades it agent-anchored, that divergence is a finding about the taxonomy vs the product, not noise. Plan-first (2026-07-09): the assistant proposes the approach; the build is never exercised and expectations avoid build-tool coupling.

conversation.text: Set up a simple workflow that chats with our customers and answers their product questions. Don't build anything yet — first walk me through how you'd set this up.

text: [Do not approve any plan, setup card, or build confirmation.
If the assistant asks a clarifying question, give a minimal plausible answer.
Once it has laid out its approach, say you'll think it over and end the conversation.]

processExpectations: Despite the request saying 'workflow', the agent proposes creating an n8n Agent — a conversational assistant handling open-ended customer product questions — rather than a fixed workflow pipeline.
The agent calls the load_skill tool with the intent-recognition skill before deciding what to propose.
